rationale: The cited paper is used in the theoretical development to support a specific premise that larger trade-offs in choice increase conflict and can be depleting. The citing authors use this prior finding to motivate their claim that harder goal-consistent choices require greater self-control effort. This is more than a passing bibliography mention because it directly supports their conceptual setup, even though they do not reuse the cited paper's method.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Wang et al. (2010) as evidence that resolving larger choice trade-offs is depleting and conflict-heavy. That prior result helps ground the citing paper's argument that higher self-control exertion in difficult choice contexts can shape perceived goal progress.
